1	"""Pure contracts describing what a speculative training algorithm consumes.
2	
3	This module intentionally contains no factories, model classes, or runtime
4	objects.  Algorithm implementations live behind provider ports; deployment and
5	transport are resolved by the application layer.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import re
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, fields, is_dataclass
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import FrozenSet, Iterable, Tuple
14	
15	_ALGORITHM_NAME = re.compile(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$")
16	
17	
18	def _normalized_names(
19	    values: Iterable[str],
20	    *,
21	    field_name: str,
22	    allow_empty: bool = False,
23	) -> FrozenSet[str]:
24	    if isinstance(values, str):
25	        raise TypeError(f"{field_name} must be an iterable of names, not a string")
26	    normalized = frozenset(values)
27	    if not normalized and not allow_empty:
28	        raise ValueError(f"{field_name} must not be empty")
29	    invalid = [
30	        repr(value)
31	        for value in normalized
32	        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value or value.strip() != value
33	    ]
34	    if invalid:
35	        raise ValueError(
36	            f"{field_name} must contain non-empty names without surrounding "
37	            f"whitespace, got {', '.join(sorted(invalid))}"
38	        )
39	    return normalized
40	
41	
42	def _assert_pure_value(value: object, *, path: str) -> None:
43	    """Reject executable or opaque state recursively from public contracts."""
44	
45	    if isinstance(value, type) or callable(value):
46	        raise TypeError(f"{path} must be a pure value, got executable {value!r}")
47	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, Enum)):
48	        return
49	    if isinstance(value, (tuple, list, set, frozenset)):
50	        for index, item in enumerate(value):
51	            _assert_pure_value(item, path=f"{path}[{index}]")
52	        return
53	    if isinstance(value, dict):
54	        for key, item in value.items():
55	            _assert_pure_value(key, path=f"{path}.key")
56	            _assert_pure_value(item, path=f"{path}[{key!r}]")
57	        return
58	    if is_dataclass(value) and not isinstance(value, type):
59	        for field in fields(value):
60	            _assert_pure_value(
61	                getattr(value, field.name),
62	                path=f"{path}.{field.name}",
63	            )
64	        return
65	    raise TypeError(
66	        f"{path} must contain only serializable contract values, got "
67	        f"{type(value).__name__}"
68	    )
69	
70	
71	class FeatureMode(str, Enum):
72	    """How algorithm-ready target features are consumed."""
73	
74	    OFFLINE = "offline"
75	    STREAMING = "streaming"
76	
77	
78	@dataclass(frozen=True)
79	class DraftRequirement:
80	    """Pure compatibility requirements for a draft architecture.
81	
82	    Draft configuration loading, target-derived generation, validation, and
83	    model construction belong to the draft-model registry and algorithm-owned
84	    providers.  Only stable identifiers and declarative override names belong
85	    here.
86	    """
87	
88	    compatible_architectures: FrozenSet[str]
89	    default_architecture: str
90	    supported_overrides: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
91	    fixed_override_values: Tuple[Tuple[str, int], ...] = ()
92	
93	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
94	        architectures = _normalized_names(
95	            self.compatible_architectures,
96	            field_name="compatible_architectures",
97	        )
98	        overrides = _normalized_names(
99	            self.supported_overrides,
100	            field_name="supported_overrides",
101	            allow_empty=True,
102	        )
103	        if self.default_architecture not in architectures:
104	            raise ValueError(
105	                "default_architecture must be present in " "compatible_architectures"
106	            )
107	        fixed_values = tuple(self.fixed_override_values)
108	        fixed_names = [name for name, _value in fixed_values]
109	        duplicates = sorted(
110	            name for name in set(fixed_names) if fixed_names.count(name) > 1
111	        )
112	        if duplicates:
113	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate fixed override values: {duplicates}")
114	        unsupported = sorted(set(fixed_names) - overrides)
115	        if unsupported:
116	            raise ValueError(
117	                "fixed_override_values must name supported overrides, got "
118	                f"{unsupported}"
119	            )
120	        if any(
121	            not isinstance(name, str)
122	            or isinstance(value, bool)
123	            or not isinstance(value, int)
124	            for name, value in fixed_values
125	        ):
126	            raise TypeError("fixed_override_values must contain (name, integer) pairs")
127	        object.__setattr__(self, "compatible_architectures", architectures)
128	        object.__setattr__(self, "supported_overrides", overrides)
129	        object.__setattr__(self, "fixed_override_values", fixed_values)
130	
131	
132	@dataclass(frozen=True)
133	class OfflineStorageContract:
134	    """Raw offline record shape before an algorithm normalizer is applied."""
135	
136	    format: str
137	    required_tensors: FrozenSet[str]
138	    normalizer: str
139	    schema_version: int = 1
140	
141	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
142	        for field_name in ("format", "normalizer"):
143	            value = getattr(self, field_name)
144	            if not isinstance(value, str) or not value or value.strip() != value:
145	                raise ValueError(
146	                    f"{field_name} must be a non-empty name without surrounding "
147	                    "whitespace"
148	                )
149	        if self.schema_version != 1:
150	            raise ValueError("only offline storage schema_version=1 is supported")
151	        object.__setattr__(
152	            self,
153	            "required_tensors",
154	            _normalized_names(
155	                self.required_tensors,
156	                field_name="required_tensors",
157	            ),
158	        )
159	
160	
161	@dataclass(frozen=True)
162	class FeatureContract:
163	    """Algorithm-ready tensors for one ``(mode, modality)`` pair."""
164	
165	    mode: FeatureMode
166	    modality: str
167	    required_tensors: FrozenSet[str]
168	    optional_tensors: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
169	    allowed_target_representations: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
170	    default_target_representation: str | None = None
171	    schema_version: int = 1
172	    storage: OfflineStorageContract | None = None
173	
174	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
175	        try:
176	            mode = FeatureMode(self.mode)
177	        except ValueError as exc:
178	            raise ValueError(f"unsupported feature mode {self.mode!r}") from exc
179	        if (
180	            not isinstance(self.modality, str)
181	            or not self.modality
182	            or self.modality.strip() != self.modality
183	        ):
184	            raise ValueError(
185	                "modality must be a non-empty name without surrounding whitespace"
186	            )
187	        if self.schema_version != 1:
188	            raise ValueError("only feature schema_version=1 is supported")
189	        required = _normalized_names(
190	            self.required_tensors,
191	            field_name="required_tensors",
192	        )
193	        optional = _normalized_names(
194	            self.optional_tensors,
195	            field_name="optional_tensors",
196	            allow_empty=True,
197	        )
198	        representations = _normalized_names(
199	            self.allowed_target_representations,
200	            field_name="allowed_target_representations",
201	            allow_empty=True,
202	        )
203	        overlap = required & optional
204	        if overlap:
205	            raise ValueError(
206	                "required_tensors and optional_tensors must be disjoint, got "
207	                f"{sorted(overlap)}"
208	            )
209	        if representations:
210	            if self.default_target_representation not in representations:
211	                raise ValueError(
212	                    "default_target_representation must be present in "
213	                    "allowed_target_representations"
214	                )
215	        elif self.default_target_representation is not None:
216	            raise ValueError(
217	                "default_target_representation requires at least one allowed "
218	                "target representation"
219	            )
220	        if mode is FeatureMode.OFFLINE and self.storage is None:
221	            raise ValueError("offline feature contracts require a storage contract")
222	        if mode is FeatureMode.STREAMING and self.storage is not None:
223	            raise ValueError("streaming feature contracts cannot define storage")
224	        object.__setattr__(self, "mode", mode)
225	        object.__setattr__(self, "required_tensors", required)
226	        object.__setattr__(self, "optional_tensors", optional)
227	        object.__setattr__(self, "allowed_target_representations", representations)
228	
229	    @property
230	    def key(self) -> tuple[FeatureMode, str]:
231	        return self.mode, self.modality
232	
233	
234	@dataclass(frozen=True)
235	class AlgorithmCapabilities:
236	    """Static algorithm constraints independent of deployment topology."""
237	
238	    attention_backends: FrozenSet[str]
239	    required_batch_size: int | None = None
240	    supports_compact_teacher: bool = False
241	    supports_vocab_mapping: bool = False
242	    allows_aux_layer_override: bool = False
243	
244	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
245	        attention_backends = _normalized_names(
246	            self.attention_backends,
247	            field_name="attention_backends",
248	        )
249	        if self.required_batch_size is not None and (
250	            isinstance(self.required_batch_size, bool)
251	            or not isinstance(self.required_batch_size, int)
252	            or self.required_batch_size <= 0
253	        ):
254	            raise ValueError("required_batch_size must be a positive integer or None")
255	        for field_name in (
256	            "supports_compact_teacher",
257	            "supports_vocab_mapping",
258	            "allows_aux_layer_override",
259	        ):
260	            if not isinstance(getattr(self, field_name), bool):
261	                raise TypeError(f"{field_name} must be a bool")
262	        object.__setattr__(self, "attention_backends", attention_backends)
263	
264	
265	@dataclass(frozen=True)
266	class AlgorithmSpec:
267	    """Complete topology-free, executable-free contract for one algorithm."""
268	
269	    name: str
270	    draft: DraftRequirement
271	    feature_contracts: Tuple[FeatureContract, ...]
272	    capabilities: AlgorithmCapabilities
273	
274	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
275	        if not isinstance(self.name, str) or not _ALGORITHM_NAME.fullmatch(self.name):
276	            raise ValueError(
277	                "algorithm name must start with a lowercase letter and contain "
278	                "only lowercase letters, digits, '_' or '-'"
279	            )
280	        if not isinstance(self.draft, DraftRequirement):
281	            raise TypeError("draft must be a DraftRequirement")
282	        if not isinstance(self.capabilities, AlgorithmCapabilities):
283	            raise TypeError("capabilities must be AlgorithmCapabilities")
284	        contracts = tuple(self.feature_contracts)
285	        if not contracts:
286	            raise ValueError("feature_contracts must not be empty")
287	        invalid = [
288	            type(contract).__name__
289	            for contract in contracts
290	            if not isinstance(contract, FeatureContract)
291	        ]
292	        if invalid:
293	            raise TypeError(
294	                "feature_contracts must contain FeatureContract values, got "
295	                f"{invalid}"
296	            )
297	        keys = [contract.key for contract in contracts]
298	        duplicates = sorted(
299	            (mode.value, modality)
300	            for mode, modality in set(keys)
301	            if keys.count((mode, modality)) > 1
302	        )
303	        if duplicates:
304	            raise ValueError(
305	                "feature_contracts contains duplicate (mode, modality) keys: "
306	                f"{duplicates}"
307	            )
308	        object.__setattr__(self, "feature_contracts", contracts)
309	        _assert_pure_value(self, path="AlgorithmSpec")
310	
311	    @property
312	    def modalities(self) -> FrozenSet[str]:
313	        return frozenset(contract.modality for contract in self.feature_contracts)
314	
315	    @property
316	    def feature_modes(self) -> FrozenSet[FeatureMode]:
317	        return frozenset(contract.mode for contract in self.feature_contracts)
318	
319	    @property
320	    def supports_online(self) -> bool:
321	        """Whether any streaming feature contract is declared."""
322	
323	        return FeatureMode.STREAMING in self.feature_modes
324	
325	    def supports(self, mode: FeatureMode | str, modality: str) -> bool:
326	        try:
327	            resolved_mode = FeatureMode(mode)
328	        except ValueError:
329	            return False
330	        return any(
331	            contract.key == (resolved_mode, modality)
332	            for contract in self.feature_contracts
333	        )
334	
335	    def feature_contract(
336	        self,
337	        mode: FeatureMode | str,
338	        modality: str,
339	    ) -> FeatureContract:
340	        try:
341	            resolved_mode = FeatureMode(mode)
342	        except ValueError as exc:
343	            raise KeyError(f"unknown feature mode {mode!r}") from exc
344	        for contract in self.feature_contracts:
345	            if contract.key == (resolved_mode, modality):
346	                return contract
347	        supported = sorted(
348	            (contract.mode.value, contract.modality)
349	            for contract in self.feature_contracts
350	        )
351	        raise KeyError(
352	            f"algorithm {self.name!r} does not support "
353	            f"({resolved_mode.value!r}, {modality!r}); supported: {supported}"
354	        )
355	
356	
357	__all__ = [
358	    "AlgorithmCapabilities",
359	    "AlgorithmSpec",
360	    "DraftRequirement",
361	    "FeatureContract",
362	    "FeatureMode",
363	    "OfflineStorageContract",
364	]
365

1	"""Explicit, immutable registrations for algorithm contracts and providers."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Iterable, Iterator, Tuple
7	
8	from specforge.algorithms.contracts import AlgorithmSpec
9	
10	
11	@dataclass(frozen=True)
12	class AlgorithmRegistration:
13	    """One lookup result containing pure metadata and executable providers.
14	
15	    Keeping the two halves in one registration avoids parallel spec/provider
16	    registries.  Planning reads ``spec``; the composition root alone reads
17	    ``providers``.
18	    """
19	
20	    spec: AlgorithmSpec
21	    providers: object
22	
23	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
24	        if not isinstance(self.spec, AlgorithmSpec):
25	            raise TypeError("spec must be an AlgorithmSpec")
26	        if self.providers is None:
27	            raise TypeError("providers must not be None")
28	        provider_name = getattr(self.providers, "algorithm_name", self.spec.name)
29	        if provider_name != self.spec.name:
30	            raise ValueError(
31	                "provider algorithm_name must match spec.name: "
32	                f"{provider_name!r} != {self.spec.name!r}"
33	            )
34	
35	    @property
36	    def name(self) -> str:
37	        return self.spec.name
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
41	class AlgorithmRegistry:
42	    """Immutable catalog assembled explicitly by the composition root."""
43	
44	    _registrations: Tuple[AlgorithmRegistration, ...]
45	
46	    def __init__(
47	        self,
48	        registrations: Iterable[AlgorithmRegistration] = (),
49	    ) -> None:
50	        resolved = tuple(registrations)
51	        invalid = [
52	            type(registration).__name__
53	            for registration in resolved
54	            if not isinstance(registration, AlgorithmRegistration)
55	        ]
56	        if invalid:
57	            raise TypeError(
58	                "registry values must be AlgorithmRegistration, got " f"{invalid}"
59	            )
60	        names = [registration.name for registration in resolved]
61	        duplicates = sorted(name for name in set(names) if names.count(name) > 1)
62	        if duplicates:
63	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate algorithm registrations: {duplicates}")
64	        object.__setattr__(
65	            self,
66	            "_registrations",
67	            tuple(sorted(resolved, key=lambda registration: registration.name)),
68	        )
69	
70	    @property
71	    def names(self) -> Tuple[str, ...]:
72	        return tuple(registration.name for registration in self._registrations)
73	
74	    @property
75	    def registrations(self) -> Tuple[AlgorithmRegistration, ...]:
76	        return self._registrations
77	
78	    def resolve(self, name: str) -> AlgorithmRegistration:
79	        for registration in self._registrations:
80	            if registration.name == name:
81	                return registration
82	        raise KeyError(
83	            f"unknown algorithm {name!r}; registered algorithms: {list(self.names)}"
84	        )
85	
86	    def with_registration(
87	        self,
88	        registration: AlgorithmRegistration,
89	    ) -> "AlgorithmRegistry":
90	        return AlgorithmRegistry((*self._registrations, registration))
91	
92	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[AlgorithmRegistration]:
93	        return iter(self._registrations)
94	
95	    def __len__(self) -> int:
96	        return len(self._registrations)
97	
98	
99	__all__ = ["AlgorithmRegistration", "AlgorithmRegistry"]
100

1	# Control plane design
2	
3	The control plane moves metadata only. Its public record-accepting endpoints run
4	`assert_no_tensors`; feature tensors stay behind the data-plane `FeatureStore`.
5	The whole-system topology is documented in
6	[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).
7	
8	## Responsibilities
9	
10	`DataFlowController` provides the shared metadata primitives:
11	
12	- prompt ingest, leasing, retry, and terminal failure;
13	- `SampleRef` commit deduplication;
14	- optional sample-ref lease/ack/fail primitives used by tests and internal
15	  staging, but disabled on the canonical online producer;
16	- trainer registration and optimizer-boundary ack transactions;
17	- status for active prompt, queue, and durable-ack state.
18	
19	The launch topology decides which subset is active. The controller has no run
20	loop and never calls a rollout worker or trainer.
21	
22	## Topology-specific ownership
23	
24	| Topology | Controller/ledger ownership | Reference delivery |
25	| --- | --- | --- |
26	| Colocated offline | No ledger or staging queue | Fixed, re-iterable ref list |
27	| Disaggregated offline | No training ledger; producer publishes a static manifest | Fixed, re-iterable manifest refs |
28	| Disaggregated online producer | Prompt lifecycle only; `NoOpMetadataStore`; local sample enqueue is disabled | Publishes refs directly to the shared `StreamingRefChannel` |
29	| Disaggregated online consumer | Rank 0 owns the only fresh retaining ledger; every rank owns a `DPAckController` view | `RefDistributor -> per-rank InboxChannel -> StreamingRefQueue` |
30	
31	The online-disaggregated producer therefore has no training ledger and no local
32	training queue. Deduplication and durable training state belong exclusively to
33	the consumer attempt.
34	
35	## Online-disaggregated consumer
36	
37	```mermaid
38	flowchart TD
39	  SRC[shared StreamingRefChannel] --> RD[RefDistributor on rank 0]
40	  RD -->|commit and dedup| DB[(fresh SQLite ledger)]
41	  RD --> I0[rank 0 InboxChannel]
42	  RD --> IN[rank N InboxChannel]
43	  I0 --> Q0[rank 0 StreamingRefQueue]
44	  IN --> QN[rank N StreamingRefQueue]
45	  Q0 --> T0[rank 0 trainer]
46	  QN --> TN[rank N trainer]
47	  T0 --> ACK[DPAckController collective]
48	  TN --> ACK
49	  ACK -->|rank 0 writes once| DB
50	```
51	
52	Rank 0 is the single bookkeeping authority:
53	
54	1. It rejects a non-retaining or non-fresh ledger.
55	2. It is the only process that reads the producer channel.
56	3. It commits and deduplicates refs, then dispatches only complete global
57	   optimizer windows into one inbox per rank.
58	4. It gathers each rank's optimizer-boundary sample ids through
59	   `DPAckController` and records one durable ack transaction.
60	
61	Non-authority ranks participate in the gather but never write the durable
62	ledger. After rank 0 broadcasts commit success, every rank removes only its
63	local feature ids and collectively reports cleanup failures before inbox ack.
64	One-rank runs use this exact path as well; there is no direct-channel consumer
65	branch.
66	
67	Inbox queue acknowledgements happen after each materialized micro-batch and are
68	forwarded by the distributor to the source channel's consumed counter. Durable
69	ack is separate: it happens once at the optimizer boundary and includes all
70	`dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps` sample ids. This separation keeps
71	producer backpressure responsive without weakening optimizer-step durability.
72	
73	## Handshake and termination
74	
75	Consumer rank 0 publishes
76	
77	```text
78	quantum = dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps
79	```
80	
81	before the distributor starts. The producer waits for that value and requires
82	its in-flight high watermark to be at least `quantum`. The canonical CLI uses
83	`DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK=256` when the variable is unset.
84	
85	The distributor closes rank inboxes only after the producer source is closed,
86	all source refs are drained, and no partial quantum remains. A partial quantum
87	at EOF is a terminal attempt failure: the staged refs are failed non-retryably,
88	their feature objects are aborted best-effort, and every rank receives a
89	failure sentinel rather than a clean close.
90	
91	Producer and consumer failures use distinct sentinels, so neither side can
92	interpret a peer crash as successful EOF. Early consumer completion also
93	signals the producer to stop and clean published Mooncake objects.
94	
95	## Freshness and recovery contract
96	
97	The canonical online launcher requires a new SQLite path for every fresh
98	consumer, including `dp_size=1`, and rejects an existing database, WAL, or SHM
99	file. The runtime also rejects a ledger with committed rows. Inbox files are
100	ephemeral and recreated by rank 0 for a fresh attempt.
101	
102	Online disaggregated recovery is consumer-only. With the original SQLite
103	ledger, channel/inboxes, Mooncake objects, and matching checkpoint still
104	available, rank 0 verifies the durable step, skips acknowledged refs, and
105	requeues the unacknowledged tail. A producer never resumes, and a consumed
106	stream is never iterated as a second trainer epoch. A new producer attempt must
107	use fresh channel, store-id, run-id, and output artifacts.
108	
109	The durable acknowledgement step must equal the restored checkpoint step.
110	Acknowledgements can advance after the most recent periodic checkpoint, so a
111	crash in that interval is intentionally non-resumable: recovery fails closed
112	rather than replaying consumed refs against older optimizer state.
113	
114	Offline refs remain fixed and re-iterable, so offline checkpoint resume and
115	multiple epochs keep their existing semantics.
116	
117	## Metadata stores
118	
119	- `NoOpMetadataStore` is used where no durable sample ledger is required, most
120	  importantly offline paths and the online-disaggregated producer.
121	- `InMemoryMetadataStore` supports local bookkeeping and non-authority DP
122	  controller instances.
123	- `SQLiteMetadataStore` is the canonical online-disaggregated consumer ledger.
124	  Rank 0 is its sole writer.
125	
126	Weight publishing and a weight-version registry are not implemented in this
127	control plane.
128

# Control plane design

The control plane moves metadata only. Its public record-accepting endpoints run
`assert_no_tensors`; feature tensors stay behind the data-plane `FeatureStore`.
The whole-system topology is documented in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

## Responsibilities

`DataFlowController` provides the shared metadata primitives:

- prompt ingest, leasing, retry, and terminal failure;
- `SampleRef` commit deduplication;
- optional sample-ref lease/ack/fail primitives used by tests and internal
  staging, but disabled on the canonical online producer;
- trainer registration and optimizer-boundary ack transactions;
- status for active prompt, queue, and durable-ack state.

The launch topology decides which subset is active. The controller has no run
loop and never calls a rollout worker or trainer.

## Topology-specific ownership

| Topology | Controller/ledger ownership | Reference delivery |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Colocated offline | No ledger or staging queue | Fixed, re-iterable ref list |
| Disaggregated offline | No training ledger; producer publishes a static manifest | Fixed, re-iterable manifest refs |
| Disaggregated online producer | Prompt lifecycle only; `NoOpMetadataStore`; local sample enqueue is disabled | Publishes refs directly to the shared `StreamingRefChannel` |
| Disaggregated online consumer | Rank 0 owns the only fresh retaining ledger; every rank owns a `DPAckController` view | `RefDistributor -> per-rank InboxChannel -> StreamingRefQueue` |

The online-disaggregated producer therefore has no training ledger and no local
training queue. Deduplication and durable training state belong exclusively to
the consumer attempt.

## Online-disaggregated consumer

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  SRC[shared StreamingRefChannel] --> RD[RefDistributor on rank 0]
  RD -->|commit and dedup| DB[(fresh SQLite ledger)]
  RD --> I0[rank 0 InboxChannel]
  RD --> IN[rank N InboxChannel]
  I0 --> Q0[rank 0 StreamingRefQueue]
  IN --> QN[rank N StreamingRefQueue]
  Q0 --> T0[rank 0 trainer]
  QN --> TN[rank N trainer]
  T0 --> ACK[DPAckController collective]
  TN --> ACK
  ACK -->|rank 0 writes once| DB
```

Rank 0 is the single bookkeeping authority:

1. It rejects a non-retaining or non-fresh ledger.
2. It is the only process that reads the producer channel.
3. It commits and deduplicates refs, then dispatches only complete global
   optimizer windows into one inbox per rank.
4. It gathers each rank's optimizer-boundary sample ids through
   `DPAckController` and records one durable ack transaction.

Non-authority ranks participate in the gather but never write the durable
ledger. After rank 0 broadcasts commit success, every rank removes only its
local feature ids and collectively reports cleanup failures before inbox ack.
One-rank runs use this exact path as well; there is no direct-channel consumer
branch.

Inbox queue acknowledgements happen after each materialized micro-batch and are
forwarded by the distributor to the source channel's consumed counter. Durable
ack is separate: it happens once at the optimizer boundary and includes all
`dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps` sample ids. This separation keeps
producer backpressure responsive without weakening optimizer-step durability.

## Handshake and termination

Consumer rank 0 publishes

```text
quantum = dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps
```

before the distributor starts. The producer waits for that value and requires
its in-flight high watermark to be at least `quantum`. The canonical CLI uses
`DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK=256` when the variable is unset.

The distributor closes rank inboxes only after the producer source is closed,
all source refs are drained, and no partial quantum remains. A partial quantum
at EOF is a terminal attempt failure: the staged refs are failed non-retryably,
their feature objects are aborted best-effort, and every rank receives a
failure sentinel rather than a clean close.

Producer and consumer failures use distinct sentinels, so neither side can
interpret a peer crash as successful EOF. Early consumer completion also
signals the producer to stop and clean published Mooncake objects.

## Freshness and recovery contract

The canonical online launcher requires a new SQLite path for every fresh
consumer, including `dp_size=1`, and rejects an existing database, WAL, or SHM
file. The runtime also rejects a ledger with committed rows. Inbox files are
ephemeral and recreated by rank 0 for a fresh attempt.

Online disaggregated recovery is consumer-only. With the original SQLite
ledger, channel/inboxes, Mooncake objects, and matching checkpoint still
available, rank 0 verifies the durable step, skips acknowledged refs, and
requeues the unacknowledged tail. A producer never resumes, and a consumed
stream is never iterated as a second trainer epoch. A new producer attempt must
use fresh channel, store-id, run-id, and output artifacts.

The durable acknowledgement step must equal the restored checkpoint step.
Acknowledgements can advance after the most recent periodic checkpoint, so a
crash in that interval is intentionally non-resumable: recovery fails closed
rather than replaying consumed refs against older optimizer state.

Offline refs remain fixed and re-iterable, so offline checkpoint resume and
multiple epochs keep their existing semantics.

## Metadata stores

- `NoOpMetadataStore` is used where no durable sample ledger is required, most
  importantly offline paths and the online-disaggregated producer.
- `InMemoryMetadataStore` supports local bookkeeping and non-authority DP
  controller instances.
- `SQLiteMetadataStore` is the canonical online-disaggregated consumer ledger.
  Rank 0 is its sole writer.

Weight publishing and a weight-version registry are not implemented in this
control plane.

1	# Data plane design
2	
3	The data plane owns feature storage, cross-process reference transport, and the
4	single bridge from `SampleRef` metadata to tensor-carrying `TrainBatch` objects.
5	See [`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) for the complete topology.
6	
7	## Invariants
8	
9	- `SampleRef` transport is metadata-only; tensors never travel through the
10	  controller, SQLite ledger, manifest, or JSONL channels.
11	- `FeatureStore` is the only owner of captured feature tensors.
12	- `FeatureDataLoader` resolves refs through a store, validates and collates the
13	  result, and yields the only tensor-carrying runtime contract, `TrainBatch`.
14	- Offline refs are fixed and re-iterable. Online refs are consume-once and are
15	  never replayed as a second consumer epoch; resume rebuilds or reconciles only
16	  the untrained suffix.
17	
18	## Topology map
19	
20	```mermaid
21	flowchart TD
22	  subgraph DO[offline disaggregated]
23	    ING[offline ingest producer] --> DSTORE[SharedDir or Mooncake store]
24	    ING --> MAN[static ref manifest]
25	  end
26	
27	  subgraph DON[online disaggregated]
28	    SGC[patched SGLang capture] --> MC[Mooncake store]
29	    OP[producer] --> SRC[StreamingRefChannel]
30	    SRC --> RD[RefDistributor rank 0]
31	    RD --> I0[rank 0 InboxChannel]
32	    RD --> IN[rank N InboxChannel]
33	    I0 --> Q0[rank 0 StreamingRefQueue]
34	    IN --> QN[rank N StreamingRefQueue]
35	  end
36	
37	  OFF[local offline file refs] --> DL[FeatureDataLoader]
38	  MAN --> DL
39	  Q0 --> DL
40	  QN --> DL
41	  DSTORE -.-> DL
42	  MC -.-> DL
43	  DL --> TB[TrainBatch]
44	```
45	
46	The online-disaggregated path is always
47	`RefDistributor -> per-rank InboxChannel -> StreamingRefQueue`, even when the
48	consumer has one rank. A trainer never reads the producer's source channel
49	directly.
50	
51	## Stores
52	
53	### `FeatureStore` and `LocalFeatureStore`
54	
55	[`feature_store.py`](feature_store.py) defines storage lifecycle operations:
56	`put`, `get`, `release`, `abort`, and `gc`. `LocalFeatureStore` supports
57	`file://` samples for colocated offline training. Its in-memory mode remains a
58	useful test utility but is not a canonical online topology.
59	
60	`get` returns tensors plus a lease handle. The loader clones when required and
61	then releases the handle. Offline file refs remain available for later epochs.
62	
63	`abort` removes failed or abandoned samples. Optional resident-byte limits and
64	`gc` bound leaks from stranded online objects.
65	
66	### Cross-process stores
67	
68	[`disaggregated.py`](disaggregated.py) provides the shared-directory backend
69	used by offline examples. [`mooncake_store.py`](mooncake_store.py) provides the
70	Mooncake backend used by online disaggregation and optionally by offline
71	ingestion. Both implement the same `FeatureStore` contract, so the trainer and
72	loader are transport-agnostic.
73	
74	## Reference sources
75	
76	### Offline fixed refs
77	
78	[`offline_reader.py`](offline_reader.py) points at precomputed local feature
79	files. For disaggregation, [`disagg_ingest.py`](disagg_ingest.py) publishes
80	existing feature tensors into the selected store and writes one immutable
81	manifest. The consumer waits for the producer's completion sentinel and loads
82	that fixed ref list.
83	
84	Both paths feed `FeatureDataLoader` in refs mode. They support multiple epochs
85	and an offline checkpoint can seek the next iteration to its saved sample
86	position.
87	
88	### Online-disaggregated source channel
89	
90	[`streaming_ref_channel.py`](streaming_ref_channel.py) is the append-only,
91	filesystem-backed metadata channel between producer and consumer pools. It
92	publishes explicit closed, producer-failed, consumer-done, and consumer-failed
93	sidecars. Its consumed counter is the producer's remote backpressure signal.
94	
95	The producer writes refs directly to this channel. It has no local training
96	queue and no training ledger. Tensors are already in Mooncake and never enter
97	the channel.
98	
99	### `RefDistributor`, inboxes, and `StreamingRefQueue`
100	
101	[`ref_distributor.py`](ref_distributor.py) runs once on consumer rank 0. It is
102	the sole source-channel reader and the sole process that commits refs to the
103	fresh durable ledger. It groups refs into
104	
105	```text
106	quantum = dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps
107	```
108	
109	and round-robins each complete quantum into one private `InboxChannel` per
110	rank. Each rank receives exactly `batch_size * accumulation_steps` refs, enough
111	for one lockstep optimizer step. The inbox is adapted to the loader's queue
112	protocol by `StreamingRefQueue`.
113	
114	After the loader consumes a micro-batch, `StreamingRefQueue.ack` advances that
115	inbox's consumed counter. The distributor sums rank counters and advances the
116	source-channel counter; dispatch alone does not make a ref consumed. Durable
117	optimizer ack is handled separately by `DPAckController`.
118	
119	Before producer capture, consumer rank 0 publishes `quantum` on the source
120	channel. The producer's in-flight high watermark must be at least that value;
121	`DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK` defaults to `256` in the canonical CLI.
122	
123	If source EOF arrives with fewer than one full quantum staged, the distributor
124	fails those refs non-retryably, adopts and aborts their feature-store objects,
125	settles the source counter, and closes every inbox after the aligned prefix.
126	Cleanup errors remain loud and poison all inboxes. Ranks never train a partial
127	global optimizer window.
128	
129	Those terminal tail ids remain committed-but-unacknowledged in the attempt's
130	metadata ledger after their feature objects are removed. Do not reuse a
131	successfully completed ledger containing such a tail for resume; a later
132	control-plane terminal-drop state is required before that can be supported.
133	
134	## `FeatureDataLoader`
135	
136	[`feature_dataloader.py`](feature_dataloader.py) has two input modes:
137	
138	- `refs`: a fixed, re-iterable offline list;
139	- `queue`: a rank's consume-once online `StreamingRefQueue`.
140	
141	For every ref it performs `store.get -> clone if needed -> store.release`, then
142	applies the injected per-sample transform and collator. The loader contains no
143	model-specific loss logic and no topology branch.
144	
145	Queue acknowledgement has two layers in online disaggregation. Materialization
146	only releases the feature read lease. At the optimizer boundary,
147	`DPAckController` gathers every rank's sample ids, rank 0 records one durable
148	ledger transaction, then every rank removes only its own shard through its own
149	feature-store client. A second cleanup-error collective completes before each
150	private inbox advances its exact acknowledged prefix. This ordering keeps
151	producer backpressure and restart accounting aligned with durable optimizer
152	progress.
153	
154	## Attempt lifecycle
155	
156	An online producer always claims a fresh attempt and cannot resume. A fresh
157	consumer also rejects a non-empty ledger. A consumer restart is supported only
158	when its SQLite ledger, original channel/inboxes, Mooncake objects, and matching
159	checkpoint remain available: rank 0 skips the optimizer-durable prefix and
160	requeues the unacknowledged tail. For an acked remote-rank ref, the fresh
161	authority first adopts its durable key metadata before deleting it. A second
162	pass over consumed refs remains unsupported. Producer/consumer failures are
163	propagated explicitly, and both roles run a bounded pending-remove drain that
164	fails loudly instead of hiding a hard-pinned Mooncake leak.
165	
166	Offline manifests and feature objects are intentionally stable instead. They
167	remain available for repeated epochs and checkpoint resume.

# Data plane design

The data plane owns feature storage, cross-process reference transport, and the
single bridge from `SampleRef` metadata to tensor-carrying `TrainBatch` objects.
See [`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) for the complete topology.

## Invariants

- `SampleRef` transport is metadata-only; tensors never travel through the
  controller, SQLite ledger, manifest, or JSONL channels.
- `FeatureStore` is the only owner of captured feature tensors.
- `FeatureDataLoader` resolves refs through a store, validates and collates the
  result, and yields the only tensor-carrying runtime contract, `TrainBatch`.
- Offline refs are fixed and re-iterable. Online refs are consume-once and are
  never replayed as a second consumer epoch; resume rebuilds or reconciles only
  the untrained suffix.

## Topology map

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  subgraph DO[offline disaggregated]
    ING[offline ingest producer] --> DSTORE[SharedDir or Mooncake store]
    ING --> MAN[static ref manifest]
  end

  subgraph DON[online disaggregated]
    SGC[patched SGLang capture] --> MC[Mooncake store]
    OP[producer] --> SRC[StreamingRefChannel]
    SRC --> RD[RefDistributor rank 0]
    RD --> I0[rank 0 InboxChannel]
    RD --> IN[rank N InboxChannel]
    I0 --> Q0[rank 0 StreamingRefQueue]
    IN --> QN[rank N StreamingRefQueue]
  end

  OFF[local offline file refs] --> DL[FeatureDataLoader]
  MAN --> DL
  Q0 --> DL
  QN --> DL
  DSTORE -.-> DL
  MC -.-> DL
  DL --> TB[TrainBatch]
```

The online-disaggregated path is always
`RefDistributor -> per-rank InboxChannel -> StreamingRefQueue`, even when the
consumer has one rank. A trainer never reads the producer's source channel
directly.

## Stores

### `FeatureStore` and `LocalFeatureStore`

[`feature_store.py`](feature_store.py) defines storage lifecycle operations:
`put`, `get`, `release`, `abort`, and `gc`. `LocalFeatureStore` supports
`file://` samples for colocated offline training. Its in-memory mode remains a
useful test utility but is not a canonical online topology.

`get` returns tensors plus a lease handle. The loader clones when required and
then releases the handle. Offline file refs remain available for later epochs.

`abort` removes failed or abandoned samples. Optional resident-byte limits and
`gc` bound leaks from stranded online objects.

### Cross-process stores

[`disaggregated.py`](disaggregated.py) provides the shared-directory backend
used by offline examples. [`mooncake_store.py`](mooncake_store.py) provides the
Mooncake backend used by online disaggregation and optionally by offline
ingestion. Both implement the same `FeatureStore` contract, so the trainer and
loader are transport-agnostic.

## Reference sources

### Offline fixed refs

[`offline_reader.py`](offline_reader.py) points at precomputed local feature
files. For disaggregation, [`disagg_ingest.py`](disagg_ingest.py) publishes
existing feature tensors into the selected store and writes one immutable
manifest. The consumer waits for the producer's completion sentinel and loads
that fixed ref list.

Both paths feed `FeatureDataLoader` in refs mode. They support multiple epochs
and an offline checkpoint can seek the next iteration to its saved sample
position.

### Online-disaggregated source channel

[`streaming_ref_channel.py`](streaming_ref_channel.py) is the append-only,
filesystem-backed metadata channel between producer and consumer pools. It
publishes explicit closed, producer-failed, consumer-done, and consumer-failed
sidecars. Its consumed counter is the producer's remote backpressure signal.

The producer writes refs directly to this channel. It has no local training
queue and no training ledger. Tensors are already in Mooncake and never enter
the channel.

### `RefDistributor`, inboxes, and `StreamingRefQueue`

[`ref_distributor.py`](ref_distributor.py) runs once on consumer rank 0. It is
the sole source-channel reader and the sole process that commits refs to the
fresh durable ledger. It groups refs into

```text
quantum = dp_size * batch_size * accumulation_steps
```

and round-robins each complete quantum into one private `InboxChannel` per
rank. Each rank receives exactly `batch_size * accumulation_steps` refs, enough
for one lockstep optimizer step. The inbox is adapted to the loader's queue
protocol by `StreamingRefQueue`.

After the loader consumes a micro-batch, `StreamingRefQueue.ack` advances that
inbox's consumed counter. The distributor sums rank counters and advances the
source-channel counter; dispatch alone does not make a ref consumed. Durable
optimizer ack is handled separately by `DPAckController`.

Before producer capture, consumer rank 0 publishes `quantum` on the source
channel. The producer's in-flight high watermark must be at least that value;
`DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK` defaults to `256` in the canonical CLI.

If source EOF arrives with fewer than one full quantum staged, the distributor
fails those refs non-retryably, adopts and aborts their feature-store objects,
settles the source counter, and closes every inbox after the aligned prefix.
Cleanup errors remain loud and poison all inboxes. Ranks never train a partial
global optimizer window.

Those terminal tail ids remain committed-but-unacknowledged in the attempt's
metadata ledger after their feature objects are removed. Do not reuse a
successfully completed ledger containing such a tail for resume; a later
control-plane terminal-drop state is required before that can be supported.

## `FeatureDataLoader`

[`feature_dataloader.py`](feature_dataloader.py) has two input modes:

- `refs`: a fixed, re-iterable offline list;
- `queue`: a rank's consume-once online `StreamingRefQueue`.

For every ref it performs `store.get -> clone if needed -> store.release`, then
applies the injected per-sample transform and collator. The loader contains no
model-specific loss logic and no topology branch.

Queue acknowledgement has two layers in online disaggregation. Materialization
only releases the feature read lease. At the optimizer boundary,
`DPAckController` gathers every rank's sample ids, rank 0 records one durable
ledger transaction, then every rank removes only its own shard through its own
feature-store client. A second cleanup-error collective completes before each
private inbox advances its exact acknowledged prefix. This ordering keeps
producer backpressure and restart accounting aligned with durable optimizer
progress.

## Attempt lifecycle

An online producer always claims a fresh attempt and cannot resume. A fresh
consumer also rejects a non-empty ledger. A consumer restart is supported only
when its SQLite ledger, original channel/inboxes, Mooncake objects, and matching
checkpoint remain available: rank 0 skips the optimizer-durable prefix and
requeues the unacknowledged tail. For an acked remote-rank ref, the fresh
authority first adopts its durable key metadata before deleting it. A second
pass over consumed refs remains unsupported. Producer/consumer failures are
propagated explicitly, and both roles run a bounded pending-remove drain that
fails loudly instead of hiding a hard-pinned Mooncake leak.

Offline manifests and feature objects are intentionally stable instead. They
remain available for repeated epochs and checkpoint resume.

1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	"""DraftTrainStrategy: per-draft-model required features + forward/loss + projection.
10	
11	A strategy is the only place that knows how a draft model (EAGLE3 / P-EAGLE /
12	DFlash / Domino) turns a normalized ``TrainBatch`` into a loss; ``TrainerCore``
13	stays branch-free and the strategy owns the target projection. Imports model
14	code, so it is imported by training entry points, not at package load.
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import abc
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Tuple
22	
23	import torch
24	import torch.nn as nn
25	
26	from specforge.runtime.contracts import TrainBatch
27	
28	
29	@dataclass(frozen=True)
30	class StepOutput:
31	    """Per-step result: loss + strategy-specific metrics, kept generic so
32	    per-position (TTT) and single-scalar strategies share one trainer loop."""
33	
34	    loss: torch.Tensor
35	    metrics: Dict[str, Any]
36	    ratio_metrics: Dict[str, Tuple[Any, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)
37	
38	
39	@dataclass(frozen=True)
40	class StepContext:
41	    """Training-schedule state passed into ``forward_loss`` for objectives that
42	    depend on where in training we are (e.g. Domino's decaying ``lambda_base``);
43	    most strategies ignore it."""
44	
45	    global_step: int = 0
46	    total_steps: Optional[int] = None
47	
48	
49	def linear_lambda_base(
50	    global_step: int,
51	    total_steps: int,
52	    lambda_start: float = 1.0,
53	    decay_ratio: float = 0.5,
54	) -> float:
55	    """Domino base-loss weight: linear decay from ``lambda_start`` to 0 over the
56	    first ``total_steps * decay_ratio`` steps, then 0, clamped to ``[0, 1]``.
57	    Requires a real ``total_steps`` (> 0)."""
58	    decay_steps = max(1, int(total_steps * decay_ratio))
59	    progress = min(global_step / decay_steps, 1.0)
60	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, lambda_start * (1.0 - progress)))
61	
62	
63	class DraftTrainStrategy(abc.ABC):
64	    name: str
65	    required_features: set
66	
67	    @abc.abstractmethod
68	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
69	        """The module whose parameters the optimizer/backend owns."""
70	
71	    def validate_batch(self, batch: TrainBatch) -> None:
72	        missing = {f for f in self.required_features if f not in batch.tensors}
73	        if missing:
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                f"{self.name} batch missing required features {sorted(missing)}; "
76	                f"present={sorted(batch.tensors)}"
77	            )
78	
79	    @abc.abstractmethod
80	    def forward_loss(
81	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional["StepContext"] = None
82	    ) -> StepOutput: ...
83	
84	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
85	        """Select the keys this strategy persists as draft weights."""
86	        return state_dict
87	
88	
89	def _prepare_eagle_target(
90	    *,
91	    target_head: Optional[nn.Module],
92	    target_repr: Optional[str],
93	    input_ids: torch.Tensor,
94	    target: torch.Tensor,
95	    loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
96	    device: torch.device,
97	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
98	    """Normalize EAGLE-family teacher features for a training forward.
99	
100	    Online capture already shifts logits and input IDs. Offline capture stores
101	    the target model's final hidden state, so the frozen target head owns the
102	    equivalent shift and projection to full-vocabulary logits.
103	    """
104	    if target_repr == "hidden_state":
105	        if target_head is None:
106	            raise ValueError(
107	                "target_repr='hidden_state' requires a target_head to re-run "
108	                "the lm_head projection"
109	            )
110	        input_ids, target, loss_mask = target_head.preprocess(
111	            input_ids, target, loss_mask
112	        )
113	        target = target_head(target.to(device))
114	        return input_ids.to(device), target, loss_mask.to(device)
115	    return input_ids.to(device), target.to(device), loss_mask.to(device)
116	
117	
118	class Eagle3TrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
119	    """EAGLE3 TTT strategy wrapping the existing ``OnlineEagle3Model``.
120	
121	    For ``target_repr == "hidden_state"`` the strategy re-runs the frozen
122	    ``TargetHead`` over the stored target hidden state; ``logits`` /
123	    ``pruned_logits`` are used as delivered. The ``t2d`` vocab map is applied
124	    inside ``OnlineEagle3Model.forward``.
125	    """
126	
127	    name = "eagle3"
128	    required_features = {
129	        "input_ids",
130	        "attention_mask",
131	        "loss_mask",
132	        "hidden_state",
133	        "target",
134	    }
135	
136	    def __init__(
137	        self,
138	        eagle3_model: nn.Module,
139	        *,
140	        target_head: Optional[nn.Module] = None,
141	        ploss_decay: float = 0.8,
142	        compact_teacher: bool = False,
143	        compact_teacher_chunk_size: Optional[int] = None,
144	    ) -> None:
145	        self.eagle3_model = eagle3_model
146	        self.target_head = target_head
147	        self.ploss_decay = ploss_decay
148	        self.compact_teacher = compact_teacher
149	        self.compact_teacher_chunk_size = compact_teacher_chunk_size
150	        if compact_teacher:
151	            self._validate_compact_teacher()
152	
153	    def _validate_compact_teacher(self) -> None:
154	        """Validate the offline compact-teacher contract before the first step.
155	
156	        The strategy is constructed after the vocab mapping has been loaded and
157	        after FSDP wrapping.  FSDP exposes the wrapped module as ``module``;
158	        walking that one boundary keeps validation independent of the backend
159	        while the actual forward still goes through the wrapped model.
160	        """
161	        if self.target_head is None:
162	            raise ValueError(
163	                "compact teacher requires the offline target_head; it is not "
164	                "available for online capture"
165	            )
166	
167	        model = self.eagle3_model
168	        if not hasattr(model, "draft_model") and hasattr(model, "module"):
169	            model = model.module
170	        draft_model = getattr(model, "draft_model", None)
171	        if draft_model is None:
172	            raise ValueError(
173	                "compact teacher requires an EAGLE3 model with a draft_model"
174	            )
175	
176	        from specforge.core.compact_teacher import (
177	            validate_compact_teacher_enabled,
178	            validate_vocab_mapping_consistency,
179	        )
180	
181	        target_head_weight = getattr(
182	            getattr(self.target_head, "fc", None), "weight", None
183	        )
184	        vocab_size = (
185	            int(target_head_weight.shape[0])
186	            if target_head_weight is not None and target_head_weight.dim() >= 1
187	            else int(
188	                getattr(getattr(self.target_head, "config", None), "vocab_size", 0)
189	            )
190	        )
191	        draft_vocab_size = int(
192	            getattr(
193	                getattr(draft_model, "config", None),
194	                "draft_vocab_size",
195	                int(draft_model.t2d.sum().item()),
196	            )
197	        )
198	        validate_compact_teacher_enabled(
199	            is_online=False,
200	            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
201	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
202	            t2d=draft_model.t2d,
203	            target_head_weight=target_head_weight,
204	            chunk_size=self.compact_teacher_chunk_size,
205	        )
206	        validate_vocab_mapping_consistency(draft_model.t2d, draft_model.d2t)
207	
208	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
209	        return self.eagle3_model
210	
211	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
212	        return next(self.eagle3_model.parameters()).device
213	
214	    def _prepare_target(
215	        self,
216	        target_repr: Optional[str],
217	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
218	        target: torch.Tensor,
219	        loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
220	        device: torch.device,
221	    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
222	        return _prepare_eagle_target(
223	            target_head=self.target_head,
224	            target_repr=target_repr,
225	            input_ids=input_ids,
226	            target=target,
227	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
228	            device=device,
229	        )
230	
231	    def forward_loss(
232	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
233	    ) -> StepOutput:
234	        self.validate_batch(batch)
235	        t = batch.tensors
236	        device = self._device()
237	        target_repr = batch.metadata.get("target_repr")
238	
239	        compact_kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {}
240	        if self.compact_teacher:
241	            if target_repr != "hidden_state":
242	                raise ValueError(
243	                    "compact teacher is offline-only and requires "
244	                    "target_repr='hidden_state'"
245	                )
246	            # Preserve TargetHead.preprocess's shift exactly, but do not call
247	            # TargetHead.forward: OnlineEagle3Model streams the frozen head in
248	            # vocabulary chunks from these kwargs instead.
249	            input_ids, target_hidden, loss_mask = self.target_head.preprocess(
250	                t["input_ids"], t["target"], t["loss_mask"]
251	            )
252	            input_ids = input_ids.to(device)
253	            target_hidden = target_hidden.to(device)
254	            loss_mask = loss_mask.to(device)
255	            from specforge.core.compact_teacher import build_offline_teacher_inputs
256	
257	            target, compact_kwargs = build_offline_teacher_inputs(
258	                compact=True,
259	                target_model=self.target_head,
260	                target_hidden=target_hidden,
261	                chunk_size_arg=self.compact_teacher_chunk_size,
262	            )
263	        else:
264	            input_ids, target, loss_mask = self._prepare_target(
265	                target_repr, t["input_ids"], t["target"], t["loss_mask"], device
266	            )
267	        position_ids = t.get("position_ids")
268	        (
269	            plosses,
270	            acceptance_rates,
271	            acces,
272	            acc_corrects,
273	            acc_denoms,
274	            metric_losses,
275	            metric_loss_denoms,
276	        ) = self.eagle3_model(
277	            input_ids=input_ids,
278	            attention_mask=t["attention_mask"].to(device),
279	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
280	            target=target,
281	            hidden_states=t["hidden_state"].to(device),
282	            position_ids=position_ids.to(device) if position_ids is not None else None,
283	            **compact_kwargs,
284	        )
285	        weights = [self.ploss_decay**i for i in range(len(plosses))]
286	        loss = sum(weights[i] * plosses[i] for i in range(len(plosses)))
287	        return StepOutput(
288	            loss=loss,
289	            metrics={
290	                "plosses": [p.detach() for p in plosses],
291	                "acces": [a.detach() for a in acces],
292	                "acceptance_rates": [a.detach() for a in acceptance_rates],
293	                "acc_corrects": [c.detach() for c in acc_corrects],
294	                "acc_denoms": [d.detach() for d in acc_denoms],
295	                "metric_losses": [m.detach() for m in metric_losses],
296	                "metric_loss_denoms": [d.detach() for d in metric_loss_denoms],
297	            },
298	        )
299	
300	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
301	        # The target-copied embedding is skipped only when actually frozen; a
302	        # trainable embedding must be persisted (checked on the live module —
303	        # state_dict tensors are detached and carry no requires_grad).
304	        embed_frozen = all(
305	            not p.requires_grad
306	            for n, p in self.eagle3_model.named_parameters()
307	            if "embed" in n.lower()
308	        )
309	        return {
310	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
311	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
312	            if "draft_model." in k and not (embed_frozen and "embed" in k.lower())
313	        }
314	
315	
316	class PEagleTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
317	    """P-EAGLE COD strategy wrapping ``OnlinePEagleModel``.
318	
319	    P-EAGLE consumes the same target capture as EAGLE3. The runtime schema uses
320	    the singular ``hidden_state`` name, while ``OnlinePEagleModel.forward`` uses
321	    ``hidden_states``; this strategy is the explicit boundary between them.
322	    """
323	
324	    name = "peagle"
325	    required_features = {
326	        "input_ids",
327	        "attention_mask",
328	        "loss_mask",
329	        "hidden_state",
330	        "target",
331	    }
332	
333	    def __init__(
334	        self,
335	        peagle_model: nn.Module,
336	        *,
337	        target_head: Optional[nn.Module] = None,
338	    ) -> None:
339	        self.peagle_model = peagle_model
340	        self.target_head = target_head
341	
342	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
343	        return self.peagle_model
344	
345	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
346	        return next(self.peagle_model.parameters()).device
347	
348	    def forward_loss(
349	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
350	    ) -> StepOutput:
351	        self.validate_batch(batch)
352	        tensors = batch.tensors
353	        device = self._device()
354	        input_ids, target, loss_mask = _prepare_eagle_target(
355	            target_head=self.target_head,
356	            target_repr=batch.metadata.get("target_repr"),
357	            input_ids=tensors["input_ids"],
358	            target=tensors["target"],
359	            loss_mask=tensors["loss_mask"],
360	            device=device,
361	        )
362	
363	        lengths = tensors.get("lengths")
364	        if lengths is None:
365	            # P-EAGLE is currently batch-size 1. Deriving the document length
366	            # from the padding mask prevents COD samples from treating padded
367	            # positions as part of the document in the offline path.
368	            lengths = tensors["attention_mask"].sum(dim=-1)
369	
370	        loss, model_metrics = self.peagle_model(
371	            input_ids=input_ids,
372	            attention_mask=tensors["attention_mask"].to(device),
373	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
374	            target=target,
375	            hidden_states=tensors["hidden_state"].to(device),
376	            lengths=lengths.to(device),
377	        )
378	        if not isinstance(loss, torch.Tensor) or loss.numel() != 1:
379	            raise ValueError(
380	                "peagle model must return a scalar loss tensor; "
381	                f"got {type(loss).__name__} with shape="
382	                f"{getattr(loss, 'shape', None)}"
383	            )
384	
385	        metrics = {
386	            name: value.detach() if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor) else value
387	            for name, value in model_metrics.items()
388	        }
389	        correct = metrics.get("full_acc_sum")
390	        denominator = metrics.get("full_acc_total")
391	        if correct is not None and denominator is not None:
392	            correct = torch.as_tensor(correct, device=device)
393	            denominator = torch.as_tensor(denominator, device=device)
394	            metrics["accuracy"] = correct / denominator.clamp_min(1)
395	
396	        return StepOutput(loss=loss.reshape(()), metrics=metrics)
397	
398	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
399	        # Unlike the stock EAGLE3 path, P-EAGLE trains its token embeddings and
400	        # mask_hidden parameter. Persist the complete draft model so both survive
401	        # checkpoint/resume and export.
402	        return {
403	            key.replace("draft_model.", ""): value
404	            for key, value in state_dict.items()
405	            if "draft_model." in key
406	        }
407	
408	
409	class DFlashTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
410	    """DFlash block-parallel strategy wrapping the existing ``OnlineDFlashModel``.
411	
412	    Shares the trainer/backend/loader/checkpoint spine with EAGLE3; only the
413	    per-step forward/loss differs (single block-wise pass, scalar loss, hard
414	    real-token labels — no target distribution, no vocab map). ``hidden_states``
415	    is DFlash's own schema name, distinct from EAGLE3's ``hidden_state``.
416	    """
417	
418	    name = "dflash"
419	    required_features = {"input_ids", "hidden_states", "loss_mask"}
420	
421	    def __init__(self, dflash_model: nn.Module) -> None:
422	        self.dflash_model = dflash_model
423	
424	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
425	        return self.dflash_model
426	
427	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
428	        return next(self.dflash_model.parameters()).device
429	
430	    def forward_loss(
431	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
432	    ) -> StepOutput:
433	        self.validate_batch(batch)
434	        t = batch.tensors
435	        device = self._device()
436	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.dflash_model(
437	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
438	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
439	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
440	        )
441	        metrics = {"accuracy": accuracy.detach()}
442	        if "accuracy_denom" in model_metrics:
443	            metrics["accuracy_denom"] = model_metrics["accuracy_denom"]
444	        return StepOutput(loss=loss, metrics=metrics)
445	
446	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
447	        # Everything trainable lives under draft_model.; the target
448	        # embedding/head are a separate module, not persisted as draft weights.
449	        return {
450	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
451	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
452	            if "draft_model." in k
453	        }
454	
455	
456	class DSparkTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
457	    """DSpark strategy over DFlash with target hidden-state supervision."""
458	
459	    name = "dspark"
460	    required_features = {
461	        "input_ids",
462	        "hidden_states",
463	        "loss_mask",
464	        "target_last_hidden_states",
465	    }
466	
467	    def __init__(self, dspark_model: nn.Module) -> None:
468	        self.dspark_model = dspark_model
469	
470	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
471	        return self.dspark_model
472	
473	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
474	        return next(self.dspark_model.parameters()).device
475	
476	    def forward_loss(
477	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
478	    ) -> StepOutput:
479	        self.validate_batch(batch)
480	        t = batch.tensors
481	        device = self._device()
482	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.dspark_model(
483	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
484	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
485	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
486	            target_last_hidden_states=t["target_last_hidden_states"].to(device),
487	        )
488	        metrics = {
489	            "accuracy": accuracy.detach(),
490	        }
491	        ratio_metrics = model_metrics.get("ratio_metrics", {})
492	        for name in (
493	            "accuracy_denom",
494	            "ce_loss",
495	            "l1_loss",
496	            "confidence_loss",
497	            "confidence_abs_error",
498	        ):
499	            if name in model_metrics:
500	                metrics[name] = model_metrics[name]
501	        return StepOutput(
502	            loss=loss,
503	            metrics=metrics,
504	            ratio_metrics=ratio_metrics,
505	        )
506	
507	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
508	        return {
509	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
510	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
511	            if "draft_model." in k
512	        }
513	
514	
515	class DominoTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
516	    """Domino block-parallel strategy wrapping ``OnlineDominoModel``.
517	
518	    Shares the trainer/backend/loader/checkpoint spine and feature schema with
519	    DFlash. Unlike the others, its loss blends a base loss with a weight
520	    ``lambda_base`` that decays over training, so it reads :class:`StepContext`.
521	    """
522	
523	    name = "domino"
524	    required_features = {"input_ids", "hidden_states", "loss_mask"}
525	
526	    def __init__(
527	        self,
528	        domino_model: nn.Module,
529	        *,
530	        lambda_start: float = 1.0,
531	        decay_ratio: float = 0.5,
532	    ) -> None:
533	        self.domino_model = domino_model
534	        self.lambda_start = lambda_start
535	        self.decay_ratio = decay_ratio
536	
537	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
538	        return self.domino_model
539	
540	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
541	        return next(self.domino_model.parameters()).device
542	
543	    def _lambda_base(self, ctx: Optional[StepContext]) -> float:
544	        # No schedule horizon -> pure final loss (lambda_base = 0).
545	        if ctx is None or not ctx.total_steps:
546	            return 0.0
547	        return linear_lambda_base(
548	            ctx.global_step, ctx.total_steps, self.lambda_start, self.decay_ratio
549	        )
550	
551	    def forward_loss(
552	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
553	    ) -> StepOutput:
554	        self.validate_batch(batch)
555	        t = batch.tensors
556	        device = self._device()
557	        lambda_base = self._lambda_base(ctx)
558	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.domino_model(
559	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
560	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
561	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
562	            lambda_base=lambda_base,
563	        )
564	        metrics = dict(model_metrics)
565	        metrics["accuracy"] = accuracy.detach()
566	        metrics.setdefault(
567	            "lambda_base",
568	            torch.tensor(float(lambda_base), device=loss.device),
569	        )
570	        return StepOutput(loss=loss, metrics=metrics)
571	
572	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
573	        # Everything trainable lives under draft_model.; the target
574	        # embedding/head are a separate module, not persisted as draft weights.
575	        return {
576	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
577	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
578	            if "draft_model." in k
579	        }
580	
581	
582	__all__ = [
583	    "DraftTrainStrategy",
584	    "Eagle3TrainStrategy",
585	    "PEagleTrainStrategy",
586	    "DFlashTrainStrategy",
587	    "DSparkTrainStrategy",
588	    "DominoTrainStrategy",
589	    "StepOutput",
590	    "StepContext",

592	]

9:"""Draft-architecture registry: the extension point for new draft model classes.
11:A draft *architecture* (the model class) is a separate axis from an immutable
12:algorithm registration: one architecture can train under several algorithms
13:and vice versa. Registering a class here makes it constructible from a
14:draft-config JSON whose
15:``architectures[0]`` names it — adding an architecture is a new file plus
16:``@register_draft``, not an edit to ``modeling/auto.py``.
17:"""
19:from __future__ import annotations
21:from typing import Dict, List, Optional
23:DRAFT_REGISTRY: Dict[str, type] = {}
26:def register_draft(cls: Optional[type] = None, *, name: Optional[str] = None):
27:    """Class decorator registering a draft architecture.
29:    The key defaults to the class name — exactly what draft-config JSONs carry
30:    in ``architectures``. The class must declare ``config_class`` (the HF
31:    ``PretrainedConfig`` subclass it is built from) so the auto loaders can
32:    resolve both the model and its config from the one registry entry.
33:    """
35:    def _register(cls: type) -> type:
36:        key = name or cls.__name__
37:        if getattr(cls, "config_class", None) is None:
38:            raise TypeError(
39:                f"@register_draft: {cls.__name__} must declare config_class"
40:            )
41:        existing = DRAFT_REGISTRY.get(key)
42:        if existing is not None and existing is not cls:
43:            raise ValueError(
44:                f"draft architecture {key!r} already registered to "
45:                f"{existing.__name__}"
46:            )
47:        DRAFT_REGISTRY[key] = cls
48:        return cls
50:    return _register(cls) if cls is not None else _register
53:def resolve_draft(name: str) -> type:
54:    try:
55:        return DRAFT_REGISTRY[name]
56:    except KeyError:
57:        raise KeyError(
58:            f"unknown draft architecture {name!r}; available: {available_drafts()}"
59:        ) from None
62:def available_drafts() -> List[str]:
63:    return sorted(DRAFT_REGISTRY)
66:__all__ = ["DRAFT_REGISTRY", "register_draft", "resolve_draft", "available_drafts"]

31:    ``PretrainedConfig`` subclass it is built from) so the auto loaders can
32:    resolve both the model and its config from the one registry entry.
33:    """
35:    def _register(cls: type) -> type:
36:        key = name or cls.__name__
37:        if getattr(cls, "config_class", None) is None:
38:            raise TypeError(
39:                f"@register_draft: {cls.__name__} must declare config_class"
40:            )
41:        existing = DRAFT_REGISTRY.get(key)
42:        if existing is not None and existing is not cls:
43:            raise ValueError(
44:                f"draft architecture {key!r} already registered to "
45:                f"{existing.__name__}"
46:            )
47:        DRAFT_REGISTRY[key] = cls
48:        return cls
50:    return _register(cls) if cls is not None else _register
53:def resolve_draft(name: str) -> type:
54:    try:
55:        return DRAFT_REGISTRY[name]
56:    except KeyError:
57:        raise KeyError(
58:            f"unknown draft architecture {name!r}; available: {available_drafts()}"
59:        ) from None
62:def available_drafts() -> List[str]:
63:    return sorted(DRAFT_REGISTRY)
66:__all__ = ["DRAFT_REGISTRY", "register_draft", "resolve_draft", "available_drafts"]
